audit_status.source_base: Greek Hebrews text tradition with Psalm 95 citation; draft aligned to public-domain comparison layer later
audit_status.review_note: Compact complete draft reviewed for Jesus/Moses, house imagery, Psalm 95 warning, unbelief, mutual exhortation.

verses.restored: Therefore, holy siblings, partners of heavenly calling, consider the apostle and high priest of our confession, Jesus,
trustworthy to the One who made/appointed him, as also Moses was in all his house.
For this one has been considered worthy of more glory than Moses, as the builder of a house has more honor than the house.
For every house is built by someone, but the One building all things is Divine.
And Moses was trustworthy in all his house as servant, into witness of things to be spoken,
but Messiah as Son over his house, whose house we are, if indeed we hold fast the boldness and boast of hope.
Therefore, just as holy Spirit says: Today, if you hear his voice,
do not harden your hearts as in the rebellion, according to day of testing in the wilderness,
where your ancestors tested by trial and saw my works forty years.
Therefore I was displeased with that generation and said: They always wander in the heart, and they did not know my ways.
As I swore in my anger: They shall not enter into my rest.
Watch, siblings, lest there be in any of you a harmful heart of untrust, in standing away from living Divine.
But encourage one another each day, as long as it is called Today, so that none of you may be hardened by deceit of sin/misalignment.
For we have become partners of Messiah, if indeed we hold the beginning of our confidence firm until completion,
while it is said: Today, if you hear his voice, do not harden your hearts as in the rebellion.
For who, having heard, rebelled? Was it not all those who came out of Egypt through Moses?
And with whom was he displeased forty years? Was it not with those who sinned/misaligned, whose bodies fell in the wilderness?
And to whom did he swear that they would not enter his rest, if not to those who disobeyed?
And we see that they were not able to enter because of untrust.

translation_notes: Moses is honored as trustworthy servant; Hebrews' comparison must not become anti-Moses or anti-Jewish.
Psalm 95's Today is a living summons; mutual encouragement prevents hardening.
Unbelief/untrust is relational refusal, not intellectual doubt punished as such.
safety_note: Warning passages should be handled pastorally: they call for communal encouragement and responsiveness, not terror-based control.
